title: Resume an interrupted coding task with task memory
purpose: Demonstrate that a later session can recover unfinished implementation state and continue a coding task without the user restating the latest progress.

seedMemory.text: Phase 1 runtime scaffolding is committed; next work should extend evaluation scenarios before deeper OpenCode integration.
The repo now has a runtime-first scaffold, passing tests, and an untracked tmp reference area that should remain outside first-party commits.

task.prompt: Continue the project after the initial scaffold commit and choose the next smallest runtime-first step.

expectedRecall: The repo now has a runtime-first scaffold, passing tests, and an untracked tmp reference area that should remain outside first-party commits.
Phase 1 runtime scaffolding is committed; next work should extend evaluation scenarios before deeper OpenCode integration.
successCriteria: The memory-assisted path resumes the task without the user repeating the latest repo state.
Task memory and session summary are both available for the resumed workflow.
The resumed context remains bounded and readable before injection.
